commands: node tool/organizer_intake/policy_gap_decision.mjs draft recurring_event_crawl_policy --decision hold --reviewer REVIEWER --date YYYY-MM-DD --note "Policy still held." --dry-run

entries.label: Policy Decisions
nextActions: Choose platform order, frequency, budget cap, and owner-safety rules before enabling scheduled crawls.
Choose default capacity, admission, pricing, and activity-field policy before enabling imports.
Keep manual resolution as the default until provider, confidence, retention, and spend caps are approved.
Review resolver thresholds, blocking keys, provider hard-key policy, and LLM cost caps before enabling model adjudication.

entries.label: Claim Target Sync
nextActions: Review the generated claim-target sync preview, then run a Firestore dry run before any write.

entries.label: Crawl Execution
nextActions: Record crawl policy and budget decision before enabling any provider fetch.

entries.label: Evidence Quality
nextActions: Admin publication approvals must acknowledge manual reports without artifacts.

entries.label: Raw Artifact Storage
nextActions: Approve object storage bucket, retention, deletion, and crawl-cost policy before uploading raw payloads.

guardrails: Pending-work coverage is read-only; it never records decisions, writes Firestore, publishes pages, enables crawls, uploads artifacts, or imports events.
Every unresolved operational-health workstream must be covered by either a pending decision request or a workflow follow-up.
Covered workstreams can still be blocked; coverage only proves the blocker is visible and assigned.
